claim: Golli-deficient T-cells are hyper proliferative in initial clonal expansion.

result.claim: Golli-deficient T-cells are hyper proliferative in initial clonal expansion.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 14767844, titled "Golli protein negatively regulates store depletion-induced calcium influx in T cells." The abstract of this paper explicitly states, "Golli-deficient T cells were hyperproliferative and showed enhanced calcium entry upon T cell receptor stimulation." This directly confirms the hyperproliferative nature of Golli-deficient T-cells, which is a key aspect of initial clonal expansion following T-cell receptor stimulation. While the paper has 0 citations and an unknown year, the directness of the statement provides strong support for the claim.

evidence.text: Golli-deficient T cells were hyperproliferative.
evidence.source: Golli protein negatively regulates store depletion-induced calcium influx in T cells.

evidence.text: Golli-deficient T cells showed enhanced calcium entry upon T cell receptor stimulation.
evidence.source: Golli protein negatively regulates store depletion-induced calcium influx in T cells.

evidence.text: Activation-induced cell death was increased in human CD3 gamma-deficient T cell lines.
evidence.source: Contribution of CD3g to TCR regulation and signaling in human mature T lymphocytes

evidence.text: The extended expansion of MafB/c-Maf deficient macrophages occurs without loss of differentiated phenotype and function.
evidence.source: MafB/c-Maf deficiency enables self-renewal of differentiated functional macrophages.

evidence.text: Tet-deficient MEFs cannot be reprogrammed into induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) in this study.
evidence.source: Tet and TDG mediate DNA demethylation essential for mesenchymal-to-epithelial transition in somatic cell reprogramming.

evidence.text: PDS causes deregulated G2/M progression in HR-defective cells.

evidence.text: Telomere defects (TDs) are observed in BLM-deficient cells at an elevated frequency.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited the proliferation of HER2-amplified BT474 cells exogenously expressing mutations.

evidence.text: Nac1 overexpression promotes ESC proliferation and delays ESC differentiation in the absence of leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF).

evidence.text: A high mutation rate is detected in HRR products in BCR/ABL-positive cells, but not in the normal counterparts.

evidence.text: Murine ES cells can be maintained as a pluripotent, self-renewing population by LIF/STAT3-dependent signaling.

evidence.text: Megakaryocyte (MK) is a naturally polyploid cell that gives rise to platelets.

evidence.text: Hrde-1-deficient animals exhibit steadily worsening defects in gamete formation.
